name: ADENOCARCINOMA OF SMALL BOWEL
definition: rare tumor that typically abuts but spares / only partially involves the major duodenal

demographics.Frequency: 35-40% of primary malignant neoplasms of small bowel; ~ 50 x less common than
demographics.Histo: mostly moderately to well differentiated; may arise in villous tumors / de novo; no
demographics.Location: most often in 2nd + 3rd portion = periampullary neoplasm

distribution: most often in 2nd + 3rd portion = periampullary neoplasm

CT: annular lesion with relatively short segment involvement
luminal obstruction + gastric distention ← larger tumor

clinical.Cx: progressive small bowel obstruction; intussusception; perforation (rare)
clinical.Prognosis: poor (often disseminated at time of presentation); 30% 5-year survival rate
